title: 2024 Guide: How to Source Home Furniture from China for Wholesale Buyers
author: Sarah Chen
excerpt: A comprehensive guide for international buyers and distributors on how to effectively source high-quality home furniture from Chinese manufacturers.
content:
Sourcing furniture from China has become increasingly streamlined for international buyers. In this comprehensive guide, we cover everything from finding the right manufacturer to ensuring product quality and managing logistics...

## Why Source Furniture from China?

China remains the world's largest furniture manufacturer, accounting for over 37% of global furniture exports. The combination of skilled craftsmanship, competitive pricing, and manufacturing scale makes China the top choice for international distributors.

## Step 1: Define Your Requirements

Before reaching out to manufacturers, clearly define your product specifications, target price point, MOQ requirements, and quality standards.

## Step 2: Verify Manufacturer Credentials

Always request ISO certifications, factory audit reports, and product test certificates. Reputable manufacturers will have BSCI, ISO 9001, and relevant product certifications readily available.

## Step 3: Request Samples

Never commit to bulk orders without physical samples. Evaluate construction quality, finish, hardware, and packaging carefully.

## Step 4: Negotiate Terms

Standard payment terms are 30% deposit, 70% before shipment. For established relationships, L/C at sight is also common.

## Step 5: Quality Control

Engage a third-party inspection company for pre-shipment inspection on all large orders.
seoTitle: How to Source Home Furniture from China 2024 | HomeStyle Furniture
seoDescription: Complete guide for wholesale buyers on sourcing home furniture from China. Tips on finding manufacturers, quality control, and logistics.

title: OEM vs ODM Furniture: Which Model is Right for Your Business?
author: Michael Zhang
excerpt: Understanding the difference between OEM and ODM furniture manufacturing and which model best suits different business strategies.
content:
When working with furniture manufacturers, two business models dominate: OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) and ODM (Original Design Manufacturer). Understanding the differences is crucial for making the right decision for your brand strategy.

## OEM Manufacturing

In an OEM arrangement, you provide the designs, specifications, and branding. The manufacturer produces the product to your exact specifications. This model offers maximum brand differentiation but requires significant upfront investment in design and tooling.

**Best for:** Established brands with proprietary designs, companies building long-term brand equity.

## ODM Manufacturing

With ODM, the manufacturer provides existing designs that you can rebrand and sell under your own label. This model is faster to market and requires less upfront investment.

**Best for:** Startups, Amazon/e-commerce sellers, companies entering new product categories.
seoTitle: OEM vs ODM Furniture Manufacturing | HomeStyle Furniture Guide
seoDescription: Learn the difference between OEM and ODM furniture manufacturing. Which model is right for your furniture business?

title: Top 5 Home Office Furniture Trends for 2024 Distributors
author: Lisa Wang
excerpt: The home office furniture market continues to evolve. Here are the five most important trends shaping B2B orders for 2024.
content:
The global home office furniture market reached $12.5 billion in 2023 and continues to grow. For distributors and wholesalers, understanding the key trends is essential for product selection and inventory planning.

## Trend 1: Height-Adjustable Desks

The standing desk revolution is not slowing down. Demand for electric height-adjustable desks grew 45% in 2023 and shows no signs of plateauing.

## Trend 2: Biophilic Design

Natural materials — solid wood, bamboo, natural textiles — continue to dominate consumer preferences. Products featuring sustainable materials command 15-25% price premiums.

## Trend 3: Compact Multi-Functional Furniture

Smaller living spaces are driving demand for furniture that does double duty — desks that fold into wall units, ottomans with storage, etc.

## Trend 4: Cable Management Integration

Tech-forward buyers expect built-in cable management, wireless charging pads, and USB ports integrated into furniture.

## Trend 5: Dark Color Palettes

Black, charcoal, and dark walnut finishes are trending strongly, replacing the all-white/light oak dominance of 2020-2022.
seoTitle: Top 5 Home Office Furniture Trends 2024 | HomeStyle Furniture
seoDescription: 2024 home office furniture trends for B2B distributors. Standing desks, biophilic design, and more key market insights.

title: How to Calculate Furniture Import Costs: A Buyer's Guide
author: Sarah Chen
excerpt: A practical breakdown of all the costs involved in importing furniture from China, including FOB pricing, freight, duties, and landed cost calculations.
content:
Understanding the true landed cost of imported furniture is critical for maintaining healthy margins. Many buyers focus only on the FOB price but are surprised by additional costs that can add 30-50% to the product cost.

## Components of Landed Cost

**1. FOB Price** — Factory price + export packing + inland freight to port

**2. Ocean Freight** — Typically $1,800-$3,500 per 20ft container, $2,800-$5,000 per 40ft container (varies by route)

**3. Marine Insurance** — Typically 0.3-0.5% of CIF value

**4. Import Duties** — Varies significantly by country and HS code. US: 0-25%, EU: 0-5.6%

**5. Port Handling & Customs Clearance** — $300-$600 per shipment

**6. Inland Freight** — From port to warehouse

## Sample Cost Calculation

For 100 desks at $80 FOB, 40ft container:
- FOB total: $8,000
- Ocean freight: $3,200
- Insurance: $34
- US import duty (5%): $567
- Customs: $400
- Total landed: ~$12,200 ($122/desk)
seoTitle: Calculate Furniture Import Costs from China | HomeStyle Furniture
seoDescription: Complete guide to calculating furniture import costs from China. FOB pricing, freight, duties, and landed cost explained.
